scenario: Stellar Retailer runs Camunda 8.8 with 22000 orders per day. After confirming a batch retry on 600 stuck process instances in Operate, an operator needs visibility into how the broker is progressing through the requested operations on the cluster so the team can decide when to escalate or re-run any leftover instances.
question: Where in Operate can the operator monitor the current status of the confirmed batch operation?

options.text: The incidents dashboard on the home page lists each instance the batch touched and refreshes the row colour as the broker processes each request across the cluster nodes
The cluster health view in Console reports per-partition counters for completed retries so the operator can confirm the batch finished on the broker side of the platform
The process instance detail page shows a progress bar at the top right that fills as the broker works through the batch operation on each instance in the selection
The Operations panel in Operate displays the current status of all operations, including the batch that was just confirmed on the selected process instances by the operator

a.text: Incorrect. The incidents dashboard summarizes incidents; tracking submitted batch operations and their progress is the role of the Operations panel after confirmation.

b.text: Incorrect. Console reports cluster health, but Operate itself shows the status of submitted operations through the Operations panel attached to the instance views.

c.text: Incorrect. The process instance detail page surfaces incidents and variables for a single instance; it does not aggregate progress for a multi-instance batch operation.

d.text: Correct. After confirmation, the Operations panel displays the current status of all operations, which includes any batch retry or cancellation the operator has just applied to a selection of process instances.

explanation: Operate exposes an Operations panel that reports the current status of all operations after confirmation. This is the surface where progress of a batch retry or cancellation submitted from a selection is observed.
